input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Check if .NET is already installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# Install via winget (discovers latest stable .NET 8/9 SDK)
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes Android, Windows sub-workloads)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target needs WinUI3/WindowsAppSDK)

```powershell
# Option A: Full Visual Studio (recommended — includes designer, hot reload)
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Then add required components via the VS Installer CLI:
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK.Cs `
  --quiet --norestart

# Option B: If you only want CLI builds (no VS IDE), install Build Tools:
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

## 4. Android SDK Setup

The MAUI workload bundles Android SDK pieces, but you may need to accept licenses and verify paths:

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK was installed by the workload
$androidSdkPath = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA "Microsoft\Android\sdk"
if (!(Test-Path $androidSdkPath)) {
    # Fallback: check common Android Studio path
    $androidSdkPath = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA "Android\Sdk"
}
Write-Host "Android SDK path: $androidSdkPath"

# List installed Android platforms & build-tools
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list | Select-String "Installed"

# Accept all licenses (required before first build)
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses

# If cmdline-tools aren't present, install them:
dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies -f net9.0-android `
  -p:AndroidSdkDirectory="$androidSdkPath" `
  -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true
```

## 5. Install OpenJDK (Android builds need it)

```powershell
# MAUI workload typically bundles Microsoft OpenJDK — verify:
dotnet --info | Select-String -Pattern "Java|java|JAVA"

# If missing, install via winget (discover available versions dynamically):
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17

# Set JAVA_HOME if not auto-set
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME",
  "C:\Program Files\Microsoft\jdk-17", "User")
```

## 6. Create & Build a Test Project

```powershell
# List MAUI templates to confirm installation
dotnet new list maui

# Create a new project
dotnet new maui -n MauiTestApp
cd MauiTestApp

# Build for Windows (WinUI3)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android
```

## 7. Run on Each Target

```powershell
# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Create Android emulator (if none exists)
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" list device
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64"

# Start emulator
Start-Process "$androidSdkPath\emulator\emulator.exe" -ArgumentList "-avd Pixel_MAUI"

# Deploy to emulator
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

## 8. Discover Target Framework Versions Dynamically

```powershell
# See what TFMs your SDK supports
dotnet --info

# List installed Android API levels
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list 2>$null |
  Select-String "platforms;android-"

# List installed Windows SDK versions (for the TFM suffix)
Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files (x86)\Windows Kits\10\Include" |
  Select-Object Name | Sort-Object Name -Descending
```

## Troubleshooting Checklist

| Symptom | Fix |
|---|---|
| `XA0030` license error | Re-run `sdkmanager --licenses` |
| Windows build fails with MSIX error | Install Windows App SDK: `winget install Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK` |
| Emulator won't start | Enable Hyper-V: `Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All` |
| `NETSDK1147` workload missing | `dotnet workload restore` in the project dir |

---

**Key point:** `dotnet workload install maui` does the heavy lifting — it pulls Android SDK, build tools, and platform dependencies automatically. The commands above let you verify and fill any gaps.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands right now, so they want all the PowerShell commands laid out. Let me provide a thorough, practical guide.
